## Page 3

Universal Registration Document
Including the 2024 annual financial report approved
by the Board of Directors on 19 February 2025.
This is a translation into English of the Universal registration document of the Company
issued in French and it is available on the website of the Company.
This Universal Registration Document, including the annual financial report, is a reproduction of the official
version of the Universal Registration Document prepared in XHTML and is available on the Renault Group
website (www.renaultgroup.com) (e-accessible version available on the Company’s website)
The Universal Registration Document was filed on 13 March 2025 with the French Financial Markets Authority (AMF) in its
capacity as the competent authority under the provisions of Regulation (EU) 2017/1129, without prior approval in accordance
with article 9 of the aforementioned regulation.
The Universal Registration Document may be used for the purposes of an offer of securities to the public or the admission of
financial securities to trading on a regulated market if it is supplemented by a prospectus and, where appropriate, a
summary and all the amendments made to the Universal Registration Document. These documents are then all approved by
the AMF, in compliance with (EU) regulation no. 2017/1129.

Message from

## Page 7

Renault Group
Renault Group enjoyed the best results in its history in 2024. And this despite
the international environment, which was marked by the persistence of
geopolitical divisions and uncertainties, the growing protectionist pressure
from major regional blocs, as well as the spectacular rise of the Asian
industry in the electric vehicle market.
I’d like to thank and congratulate Luca de Meo and his management team,
the architects of this remarkable performance. My gratitude also goes to the
Board of Directors for its work of listening, analysing, validating and
supporting the Group’s strategic decisions. I’d like to end by acknowledging
the commitment and efforts of every employee, who enabled Renault Group
to stand out in a particularly challenging environment for the automotive
sector.
If our company stands out, it’s because it has managed to continue the
ambitious deployment of its powerful strategy. The Group’s 10 new vehicles
were enthusiastically welcomed by journalists and customers alike.
Furthermore, the new organisation is yielding results, supported by a culture
of innovation and openness that progressed even further in 2024.
A few years ago, the company and the Alliance were fighting for survival.
I remember saying, at the height of the crisis in 2020, “One day, Renault will
regain the place it deserves”. Today, we are held up as an example, and the
cooperation with our partner Nissan has been refocused on vehicle projects,
in a spirit of agile and free partnership for the benefit of both partners.
2025 will be an intense year, as will all the years marking the transition to
electric. The way the company was able to recover in such a short period is a
great source of confidence. To anchor our success for the long term and
escape the fate of cycles, we will continue to progress in agility and initiative
and continue to spread a culture of empowerment, which is essential for
motivation and sustainable performance.
Together with the board members, I am committed to continuing to serve the
Group, so it can remain at the forefront of sustainable mobility.
I’d like to end by
acknowledging the
commitment and efforts of
every employee, who enabled
 Renault Group to stand out in
a particularly challenging
environment for the
automotive sector.
 Renault Group    I    Universal registration document 2024

## Page 8

Message from
Luca de Meo
Luca de Meo
CEO, Renault Group
6
Renault Group    I    Universal registration document 2024
www.renaultgroup.com

## Page 9

Renault Group
Who would have thought it? A company that was on the brink of collapse less
than four years ago now finds itself on the podium of European
manufacturers, while those who seemed untouchable just yesterday are
now sounding the alarm? Yet, here we are, on the threshold of 2025, which
promises to be yet another year full of twists and turns.
It’s true that the automotive industry is in a situation not seen in the last
150  years. The ongoing storm challenges the benchmarks that we once
thought were the most firmly established. The challenge posed by the new
competition from the West and, increasingly, from the East, is becoming
more evident every day. Of course, the existence of a level playing field is
essential for competition to play its role as a driving force, as it always has
since the beginning of our industry. But the most important thing lies
elsewhere: anyone who visits China today cannot help but recognise the
excitement that drives the entire ecosystem. Excellence, obsession with user
experience, speed, innovation, passion for cars: not a single ingredient is
missing! The situation is clear: the automobile industry’s centre of gravity has
shifted at a breathtaking speed.
For the car enthusiast that I am, this is excellent news: by forcing us to
question ourselves, the emergence of Chinese automobiles will pull us all up.
We have the extraordinary privilege of being here at a moment when
technological shifts, particularly in electric vehicles and software, offer us
the opportunity to reinvent what the automobile is. The ball is in our court!
In this new environment, the good news is that Renault is now in a position to
seize the opportunity. The Group benefits from the revolutionary
organisation set up over the past two years, designed to capture the new
sources of value in the sector, from electric vehicles and software to the
circular economy, new mobility services and premium vehicles, not to
mention combustion engines and hybrids. There can be no doubt about it:
the strategy has proved its worth.
Renault is therefore without hesitation to take on the challenge. Improve,
catch up, innovate, this is the roadmap. First, improve, by being more efficient
and faster, for example by doubling the speed of execution of the company’s
major processes to meet the challenge of competitiveness. Catch up, by
tapping into the Chinese ecosystem to capture its best elements and bring
them back into our fold. André Citroën, Louis Renault and Giovanni Agnelli did
the same thing at the start of the last century by drawing inspiration from
Henry Ford’s methods in the United States, as he revolutionised the industry
with the invention of the production line. Against the prevailing pessimism in
Europe, it’s worth remembering that our predecessors also had to deal with
tough competitors and that they managed to come out on top. Lastly,
innovate, because copying isn’t everything. The radical volatility in which we
find ourselves today in terms of technology, geopolitics and market conditions
sweeps away all certainties, except one: in an unpredictable environment, it is
the most agile, the one who can pivot the quickest and invent the fastest
solutions to unexpected problems while staying aligned with customer
expectations, who will come out on top. Technological innovation is now the
most precious raw material in our industry. We’re putting it at the heart of
Renault so that it drives the whole company. In the automotive world,
everything starts and ends with cars: that’s why we’re redesigning our entire
innovation strategy, focusing it on seven flagship projects: seven physical
objects, carried by cross-functional teams obsessed with customer feedback.
In a world more dynamic than ever, Renault has the advantage of being well
positioned among European manufacturers. Who could doubt it, at a time
when we’re developing the future Twingo in 2 years, thanks to Ampere? What
would have seemed like science fiction not long ago, Renault has made it a
reality. But the greatest strength of this company remains the enthusiasm
and passion of its 100,000 employees, who, after facing an existential crisis,
are now the biggest hope for the European automotive industry to build a
future worthy of it.
But the greatest strength
of this company remains
the enthusiasm and passion
of its 100,000 employees,
who, after facing
an existential crisis,
are now the biggest
hope for the European
automotive industry
to build a future worthy of it.

New financial objectives*
Financial outlook: reaching new heights!
Operating margin
Free cash flow (1)

Commitment and ambition based on the emission standards known to date.
(1)
Automotive operational free cash flow: cash flows after interest and tax (excluding dividends received from publicly listed companies) minus tangible and intangible investments net of
disposals +/- change in the working capital requirement.
(2)
ROCE = </pc>Automotive operating margin (1- average tax rate) / (PP&E + intangible assets + financial assets – investments in RCI/Nissan/Daimler + WCR).
Plan
Strategic
From volume to value creation
A plan structured in three phases that are launched in parallel, which will restore competitiveness by: 
●improving the efficiency of functional divisions through strict cost control;
●taking advantage of the Group’s industrial strengths and position as leader in electric vehicles throughout Europe;
●exploring even further into the world of data, mobility and energy services;
●relying on five businesses specialising in new value chains to become a next-generation automotive group. This
organisation has been in place since 2023.

Resurrection
The Resurrection phase of our plan is over.
As early as 2022, Renault Group achieved its profitability target
initially set for 2025.
Renault Group generated €2.1 billion in free cash flow in 2022, for
a cumulative free cash flow of €3 billion between 2021 and 2022.
Renault Group generated €2.9 billion in free cash flow in the
2024 financial year after exceeding €3 billion in 2023.

R&D
expenditure
remained
contained
under
8% of turnover.
Resurrection
is achieved almost three years ahead
Initial outlook
Achievements
Renovation
The Renovation phase, a phase linked to the introduction of a
new range of products on the market, is now well underway.
Renault Group has launched 15 new vehicles on the market
since 2022 and will launch 7 in 2025 in the most profitable and
relevant segments. 50% of these launches are in the C segment
and above, and around 50% are 100% electric vehicles.
Revolution
At the end of 2022, significantly ahead of its initial targets,
Renault Group opened the third chapter of the Renaulution plan:
Revolution.
The guiding principles of this approach focused on value and
the ecosystem are:
●
strategic focus;
●
efficiency;
●
optimised capital allocation;
●
selection of the best partners;
●
Asset-light (1) by design.
Renault Group is forging ahead with its own Revolution by
creating “5 businesses”, targeted with specialised teams, each
built on a uniform set of technologies with its own dedicated
governance and statement of income.
These businesses are as follows:
●
Ampere: the 1st electric and software pure player born from
the transformation of a traditional carmaker;
●
Alpine: an exclusive, zero-emission, global brand with motor
racing in its DNA. A unique asset-light model combined with
proprietary technologies;
●
Mobilize: built around a first-class financial captive to
address the new mobility, energy and data services market;
●
The
Future
Is
NEUTRAL:
the
automotive
industry’s
1st company dedicated to the 360° circular economy: from the
closed loop of materials to battery recycling;
●
Power: Renault Group’s traditional core business will
continue to develop innovative low-emission combustion
and hybrid vehicles under the Renault, Dacia and Renault
VUL (light commercial vehicles) brands, each with its own
dedicated organisation and governance.

## Page 16

Progress
of the strategic plan
HORSE Powertrain Limited,
a leading company in hybrid and internal combustion powertrains
Renault Group and Geely, following the agreements signed on 11 July 2023, and after approval from the relevant authorities, officially announced the
creation of HORSE Powertrain Limited on 31 May 2024, each with a 50% stake (leading to the deconsolidation of HORSE from the same date).
On 2 December 2024, HORSE Powertrain Limited finalised the acquisition of a 10% stake by Aramco, valuing the company at €7.4 billion. Renault Group
and Geely each retained a 45% stake.
HORSE Powertrain designs, develops, manufactures, and sells all solutions and systems for engines, transmissions, hybrid systems and batteries.
HORSE Powertrain aims to become a unique, top-tier partner offering cutting-edge technologies at a competitive cost, contributing to the reduction of
global vehicle emissions.
With this project, both Renault Group and Geely benefit from immediate economies of scale, better market coverage and a complementary product
portfolio and regional footprint, which allows them to offer solutions for 80% of a growing global powertrain market.
Thanks to the intellectual property transfer from Renault Group and Geely, HORSE Powertrain is autonomous in developing future engine and
transmission technologies that the market will need, particularly in the field of alternative fuels such as green methanol, ethanol and hydrogen.
Aramco’s investment will accelerate these developments. Furthermore, as part of the deal, Aramco and its subsidiary, Valvoline Global Operations, will
collaborate with HORSE Powertrain on innovation in thermal engines, fuels and lubricants.
HORSE Powertrain already serves numerous industrial customers, including Renault Group, Geely Auto, Volvo Cars, Proton, Nissan, and Mitsubishi
Motors Company. The company is ready to serve all customers and partners worldwide, offering them comprehensive solutions in powertrain
technologies and welcoming partners to strengthen the value chain coverage.
Ampere makes a difference in smart electric vehicles
Renault Group created Ampere, Europe’s only pure player in electric vehicles and software, with the aim of making electric vehicles widely
available in Europe faster than its competitors.
From the outset, Renault Group’s strategic approach was to build Ampere as an independent entity, with a team entirely dedicated to
electric vehicles and software in order to be more competitive, agile and innovative. Since 1 November 2023, Ampere has been operating
independently, and the Group is measuring the operational and industrial benefits of this new organisation day by day.
In 2022, Renault Group had announced its intention to list this entity on the stock exchange. Given the market conditions and higher-than-expected
cash-flow levels, Renault Group announced at the end of January 2024 its decision to cancel the initial public offering (IPO) process for Ampere.
In a demanding yet growing electric vehicle market over the coming years (around 25% per year over the next five years), Ampere is moving quickly to
lead the transition to electric vehicles in Europe. Over the past twelve months, Ampere has made significant progress, including the following highlights:
Ampere has accelerated its development and manufacturing times: Twingo will be developed in less than two years, at a sales price below €20,000,
and Renault 5 will be manufactured in less than 10 hours within Ampere ElectriCity.
After a successful first project with its Compact EV vehicle, Nissan asked Ampere to explore the development of its next electric model in segment A,
building on the process transformations put in place. This new opportunity demonstrates the growing appeal of Ampere as a technological platform for
other major brands: Alpine with the A290 and A390, Mitsubishi with its upcoming electric C-SUV.
To accelerate the development of its vehicles and offer the best technologies and customer experience, Ampere now relies on a team in China working
closely with its Chinese partners, with the goal of improving its practices and learning from them. Proof of its agility in times of volatility and its ability to
significantly reduce costs, Ampere is ready to integrate LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate) chemistry into its cars from early 2026 and is developing cutting-
edge cobalt-free battery technology by 2028.
Ampere will offer the first European SDV (Software-Defined Vehicle) in 2026, thanks to the horizontal approach the company is taking, particularly in
software, enabling it to shorten development times, reduce costs and retain intellectual property of what is strategic.
Ampere is on track to meet its goal of reducing its costs by 40% by 2028, between the first and second generation of electric vehicles in segment C,
thanks to LFP and new technologies such as cell-to-pack by 2026. Lastly, the next generation of Ampere electric vehicles in segment C, expected on the
market in 2028, will incorporate major innovations with the ambition to be best-in-class compared with the competition and to make up for two
generations in one.
Flexis, a joint venture for a revolutionary generation of electric vans
In 2024, Renault Group, Volvo Group and CMA-CGM (global leader in logistics across all segments of the supply chain) officially launched Flexis
SAS, a joint venture aimed at developing a new generation of 100% electric vans based on a dedicated “skateboard” platform and Software-
Defined Vehicle (SDV) architecture, with production expected to begin in 2026.

The Future Is NEUTRAL:
Renault Group and SUEZ join forces to accelerate the circular economy in the automotive sector in Europe
In October 2024, the two groups announced their decision to intensify their historic cooperation and position The Future Is NEUTRAL as a leading player
in the automotive circular economy.
SUEZ acquired a 20% stake in the capital of The Future Is NEUTRAL, alongside Renault Group, now a co-shareholder with 80%. Under this deal, The Future
Is NEUTRAL receives a €140 million investment from its two shareholders to accelerate and expand its activities for the benefit of the entire European
automotive industry.

Renault Group
Renault
Next-gen cars for living
Dacia
The essentials: plain and simple
1,577,351 vehicles sold
including 108,542 electric vehicles
676,340 vehicles sold
including 22,884 electric vehicles
Renault, the world’s best-selling French automotive brand, has
been designing popular, avant-garde “cars for living” for more
than 125 years, making cutting-edge technologies accessible to
everyone. As part of its commitment to reducing its carbon
footprint, Renault has developed a two-pronged range of
electric and hybrid vehicles, allowing everyone to move
towards the energy transition at their own pace.
Created in 1968 and relaunched by Renault Group in 2004
throughout Europe and the Mediterranean, Dacia is constantly
taking its promises of accessibility and simplicity to the next
level to offer the best value for money. Dacia is a game changer
offering simple, multipurpose, reliable cars in tune with its
customers’ needs. The brand’s five core models — Sandero,
Duster, Spring, Jogger and Bigster — are references in the
market. Present in 44 countries, Dacia has sold more than
9 million vehicles since 2004.
Alpine
At the forefront of sports innovation
Mobilize
Writing the future of mobility
4,585 vehicles sold
800,000 charging stations in Europe with
Mobilize Charge Pass
40,000 charging stations at the end of 2024
Founded in 1955 by Jean Rédélé, Alpine has established itself
over the years with its French-style sports cars. Born from
motor racing, it represents automotive excellence and
innovation for performance. With the launch of A290, Alpine
enters a new era. The era of electric sportiness in Alpine mode,
always respecting the three pillars of the brand: an
exhilarating driving experience, lightness and French know-
how. A290 is the new entry model into the Alpine world. It marks
the beginning of the 100% electric Dream Garage, where it will
soon be joined by the Sport Fastback A390 and the new A110.
Created in January 2021, Mobilize is the Renault Group brand
dedicated to new forms of mobility. It offers a complete
range for individuals, businesses, operators and regions:
financing solutions (through Mobilize Financial Services),
electric vehicle charging solutions and mobility solutions,
including the Duo and Bento quadricycles. Mobilize goes
beyond the automobile, towards a more environmentally
friendly and accessible mobility for all, towards better
energy management, and towards solutions tailored to the
needs of citizens and cities.

## Page 22

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
1.1 Renault Group
1.1.1 Purpose
“Our spirit of innovation takes
mobility further to bring people closer”
In April 2021, Renault Group unveiled its corporate purpose,
which expresses the ambition and meaning of the Group’s
collective project around the world to serve its customers
and all its stakeholders. 
Our Purpose is the bedrock on which everything lies: our
values, our strategic plan, and the course we have chosen in
terms of social and environmental responsibility. 
“A company’s Purpose is both its roots and its North Star.
The roots are what give a company depth and stability, and
the North Star is the desirable future towards which all
energy is turned”.
Jean-Dominique Senard
Chairman of the Board of Directors of Renault S.A.
We are caring, believing in responsible progress
that respects everyone. ​
Since 1898, our history has been written by passionate
people who create innovative products in tune with popular
culture and made to accompany life. We do this because
mobility is a source of fulfilment and a freedom. We believe
that this freedom goes hand in hand with preserving the
planet and living better together. So, we are taking action to
contribute to a better quality of life and limit our impact on
the climate and resources. We, too, are striving to make
progress so that safe mobility is better shared.
We are daring, embracing
the future with optimism. ​
We are a place where people can be themselves, playing
their part in a shared adventure. We are proud of our
diversity, our French roots and our international presence,
which makes us open to the world. We are strengthened by
the Alliance and by the constructive relationships we forge
with our partners. From our very beginning, our spirit of
innovation has taken us further, creating value, anticipating
mobility needs and bringing people closer.

1.2.2 Strategy and objectives
In November 2022, Luca de Meo, CEO of Renault Group and
Thierry Piéton, CFO of Renault Group, presented the third
chapter of the Renaulution strategy.
After Resurrection and Renovation, the first two phases of
the Renaulution strategic plan presented in January 2021,
Renault Group opened the third chapter and launched its
Revolution with the ambition of becoming a next-generation
automotive group.
Until now, carmakers have operated in an environment
characterised by mature combustion engine technology
and stable customer expectations. The ongoing changes
reshaping the automotive industry are leading to the
emergence of new value chains: electric vehicles (EVs),
software, new mobility services and the circular economy.
To benefit from this new environment, a new organisation
has been implemented gradually since 2023, enabling
Renault Group to capture value across all new profit pools
(estimated by external sources at around €220  billion in
2030
compared
with
€110 
billion
today).
To
seize
opportunities in these markets and adapt to the current
environment,
Renault
Group
has
created
dedicated
organisations. It is transforming its business portfolio by
exploiting value chains that are structurally more profitable.
Renault Group takes advantage of a horizontal and
ecosystem approach to co-create, co-finance and scale
strategic initiatives with leading partners.
26

General presentation of Renault Group
01
The five Businesses
These 5 Business are as follows:
1. Power: innovative low-emission combustion-engine and
hybrid vehicles
Combustion-engine and hybrid vehicles will still account for
up to 50% of global passenger car sales even by 2040. The
development of effective technologies in this area is
essential for the future of any global carmaker. This is why
Renault Group is striving to develop its core business, with
the launch of a brand-new range for Renault ICE & Hybrid
(passenger cars), Dacia and LCV – and with the creation of a
world-leading supplier of internal combustion and hybrid
engine technologies.
Thus, HORSE Powertrain Limited was officially created on
31 May 2024. Valued at €7.4 billion, the company is 45% owned
each by Renault Group and Geely while Aramco, one of the
leading companies in energy and chemicals, holds a 10% stake.
HORSE Powertrain designs, develops, manufactures, and
sells all solutions and systems for engines, transmissions,
hybrid systems (xHEV), and batteries. HORSE Powertrain
aims to become a unique, top-tier partner offering cutting-
edge technologies at a competitive cost, contributing to the
reduction of global vehicle emissions.
Renault ICE & Hybrid: global product line expansion
Despite the sharp increase in the supply of electric vehicles,
internal combustion vehicles will continue to grow,
particularly outside Europe. The Renault brand will therefore
remain present in the markets for internal combustion and
hybrid vehicles, particularly in Latin America, India, South
Korea and North Africa. Sales of Renault ICE & Hybrid
passenger cars will continue to grow by 2% per year on
average over the period 2022–2030.
To upscale in all regions, Renault continues its offensive in
the C-segment with an ambitious vehicle launch plan.
Dacia: from more than 10% operating margin to 15% in 2030
The Dacia model is unique, based on a winning combination
of three main components:
●Design-to-cost (cost efficiency) as the guiding principle,
aiming for a cost advantage of approximately -15%
compared with the competition by focusing on what really
matters to customers, the “essentials”, and capitalising on
the Group’s assets and the expertise of its 3,000 engineers.
●An unparalleled industrial and supply base with best-in-
class cost competitiveness, thanks to a high plant
utilisation rate as well as a strong level of local integration.
●A highly efficient distribution model ensuring distribution
costs around 50% lower than the Western Europe
average, with a policy of nearly no discounts and with
30% of sales coming from digital initiatives.
As a result, Dacia, which benefits from a channel mix
reaching
80-85%
for
private
customers,
is
already
generating an operating margin above 10% and aims to
reach 15% by 2030.
To achieve this ambition, Dacia, currently the leader in the B-
segment, will boldly aim for the C segment. After Jogger in
2022, Bigster will embody, in 2025, this movement towards
segment C and two other vehicles will follow, which will
double the coverage of its profit pool.
At the same time, Dacia will continue to reduce its costs and
will benefit from the doubling of volumes of the global CMF-B
platform, which will reach (all brands combined) 2 million units
by 2030.
 Renault Group    I    Universal registration document 2024
27

## Page 30

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
Dacia is helping to reinvent the internal combustion value
chain thanks to its cooperation with HORSE by developing
breakthrough engines suitable for alternative and synthetic
fuels. Dacia will gradually electrify its range in Europe by
pioneering the deployment of accessible electrical solutions.
LCV activity: propelled into the future thanks to Flexis, the
industry’s most innovative project
Renault Group’s LCV (light commercial vehicle) business is
built on solid foundations, with a European base of more
than 5 million vehicles, an ecosystem of more than 600 Pro+
dealers, 4 plants and the latest range by 2026.
To respond to a dynamic, transforming market, Renault
Group, Volvo Group and CMA-CGM (global logistics leader
across all supply chain segments) officially launched in 2024:
Flexis SAS. This joint venture aims to develop a whole new
generation of 100% electric vans based on a dedicated native
“skateboard” platform and a Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV)
architecture, with production set to begin in 2026.
Renault Group and Volvo Group each hold 45% of the capital
and plan to invest €300 million each over the next three years.
CMA CGM Group, through its PULSE energy fund, holds a 10%
stake in Flexis SAS and will invest up to €120 million by 2026.
As such, three global players in their respective industries
have joined forces with the agility of a start-up to address
the challenges of the energy transition in the light
commercial vehicle and last-mile logistics sectors.
Perfectly positioned to capture a market stimulated by the
growth of e-commerce and electrification, Flexis will offer
three competitive models for last-mile delivery and urban
logistics, with a range of up to 460  km. Thanks to this
partnership, development costs will be shared, leading to a
significant reduction in costs (over 20% of total usage costs),
and customer coverage will be maximised. Flexis will produce
its vans at Renault Group’s Sandouville plant in France and
will benefit from a low breakeven point at 35,000 units,
allowing it to be profitable from the first year of operation.
2. Ampere: THE EUROPEAN CHALLENGER: Technological
excellence and an operational focus to help bring
electric vehicles into the mainstream
Ampere is at the heart of Renault Group’s transformation.
Ampere is the smart electric vehicle specialist, a spin-off
from Renault Group. Ampere develops electric vehicles for
the Renault brand and injects electric and software
technologies for other brands (Alpine, Nissan, Mitsubishi,
etc.). Since 2023, Ampere has been an autonomous
company and the European leading electric vehicle and
software pure player.
Ampere is ready to meet the main challenges of the
transformation of the automotive industry: zero carbon
emissions and software development. It has solid assets for
which the majority of investments have already been made:
●platforms dedicated to electric vehicles;
●an industrial ecosystem (electric and software value
chains and industrial footprint);
●an attractive range of vehicles.
In
2024,
Ampere
accelerated
its
development
and
manufacturing times: Twingo will be developed in less than
two years, at a sales price below €20,000, and the Renault 5
will be manufactured in less than 10 hours within Ampere
ElectriCity.
To accelerate the development of its electric vehicles,
Ampere now relies on a dedicated entity called the
Advanced China Development Center, created to work
closely with its Chinese partners. Thanks to this integrated
team in China, Ampere aims to learn about development
processes from the Chinese ecosystem and draw valuable
lessons for its teams as well as Renault Group’s teams.
Furthermore, this will allow it to offer the best technologies
and customer experience.
Ampere’s growth profile is based on cost benefits, a
comprehensive product range and a bespoke customer
experience
Its aim is to achieve price parity between thermal and
electric vehicles before the competition, thanks to a clear
trajectory for reducing costs by 40% by 2028 between the
first and second generations of C-segment electric vehicles,
leveraging LFP technology and new technologies like cell-
to-pack by 2026.
Proof of its agility in times of volatility and its ability to
significantly reduce costs, Ampere is ready to integrate LFP
(Lithium Iron Phosphate) chemistry into its cars from early
2026 and is developing cutting-edge cobalt-free battery
technology by 2028.
It offers a full range of vehicles at the heart of the European
market: Megane E-Tech, Scenic E-Tech (awarded Car of the
Year 2024), Renault 5 E-Tech (awarded Car of the Year 2025),
Renault 4 E-Tech and Twingo E-Tech. A second generation
of vehicles will be integrated into the range in 2028. It is also
developing
complementary
activities
(vehicles
and
software) with other brands and partners such as Alpine,
Nissan and Mitsubishi Motors.
Ampere has competitive advantages beyond the product to
offer a tailor-made customer experience:
●the reputation of the Renault brand;
●a distribution network;
●an after-sales network;
●a charging ecosystem for electric vehicles;
●financing solutions.
Ampere is a story of technological excellence, made up of
innovation and partnerships, based on:
●11,000 employees, 35% of whom are engineers, 1,800 of
whom specialise in software and systems to provide
technological innovations;
●state-of-the-art solutions with OpenR Link;
●a unique horizontal approach through partnerships with
Google and Qualcomm Technologies to accelerate
solutions to market, reduce execution costs and risks and
offer customers the best solutions on the market;

●the planned launch in 2026 of the first European Software-
Defined Vehicle (SDV), on FlexEVan, thanks to the
horizontal approach the company is taking, notably in
software, allowing it to reduce costs while retaining the
intellectual property of what is strategic;;
●a next generation of Ampere electric vehicles in the
C-segment, expected on the market in 2028, will
incorporate major innovations with the ambition to be
best-in-class compared with the competition and to
make up for two generations in one.
Ampere is operational from day 1 thanks to solid assets and
the support of Renault Group:
●ElectriCity, a geographically concentrated industrial
ecosystem, at the cutting edge of productivity and with
low carbon emissions; Ampere’s industrial cluster is made
up of 4 plants, ElectriCity (Douai-Ruiz-Maubeuge) and
Cléon, at the cutting edge of technology, operational from
day one, with a total capacity of 400,000 units per year,
expandable to 620,000 units per year by 2028.75% of
suppliers are located within a 300 km radius;
●a unique ecosystem approach to cover the entire value
chain for electric vehicles, limiting investment and
retaining the ability to adapt technological choices,
thanks to partnerships with the top players in the
development of batteries and electric motors;
●clearly structured and mutually beneficial relations
between Renault Group and Ampere.
Ampere is ESG by nature
Ampere’s ESG purpose is based on three pillar:
●decarbonisation beyond electrification, aiming for a “net
zero” company by 2035;
●the circular economy to preserve resources, based on The
Future Is NEUTRAL and Mobilize;
●a just transition, with strong ambitions for human rights
and transparency in the supply chain, while being an
ethical and inclusive workplace.
3. Alpine: an exclusive, zero-emission, global brand with
motor racing in its DNA
Alpine has experienced a resurgence in recent years,
capitalising on its iconic sports coupe, A110, and its presence
at the highest level of motor sport (Formula 1, WEC - World
Endurance Championship). Today, Alpine is a true exclusive
brand, a manufacturer in its own right, asset light, focused on
technology, with a team of around 2,000 people, half of whom
are
engineers.
Alpine
also
relies
on
a
network
of
163 Alpine Stores. Being part of the Group gives Alpine access
to Ampere’s electric and software technology assets.
Alpine is developing an entirely new range (its “Dream
Garage”) that will support its growth and international
ambitions while enabling it to transition towards electric. In
2024, Alpine began expanding its range with A290, an
electric city sports car, awarded Car of the Year 2025.
The brand will continue its momentum in 2025 with the
commercialisation of the A390, a C-segment sport fastback
produced in France at the Dieppe factory. This 100% electric
vehicle
will
combine
fluidity
and
sportiness
with
spaciousness. Alpine will then unveil the next A110.
4. Mobilize: built around a leading financial captive to address
the new mobility, energy and data services market
Mobilize is built around a key asset, Mobilize Financial
Services (Mobilize F.S.), one of the best financial captives on
the market with 4  million customers. Mobilize F.S. is
extending its traditional activities while developing new
activities such as subscription, insurance and operational
leasing.
Mobilize is on track to become a cost-effective and leading
dedicated Vehicle-as-a-Service (VaaS) provider, combining
financial, mobility, energy and data services supported by
specially designed vehicles. These services, grouped
together in a single solution, will meet the needs of private
customers, fleets and mobility operators while generating
recurring revenues. What makes Mobilize stand out from
any other automotive brand is that it comes from services to
the product and not the other way around. Thanks to the
VaaS model, Mobilize will generate three times more
revenue over the entire vehicle life cycle, compared with
traditional sales.
5. The Future Is NEUTRAL: the first company in the automotive
industry dedicated to the 360° circular economy, from the
closed loop of materials to battery recycling
To anchor its commitment to the circular economy and
move forward on the path to resource neutrality, Renault
Group has created a new entity: The Future Is NEUTRAL. By
bringing together all the existing expertise of the Group and
its partners in this activity, this new entity offers closed- loop
recycling solutions at every stage of a vehicle’s life: supply
of parts and raw materials, production, use and end-of-life.
The Future Is NEUTRAL currently covers approximately 50%
of the value chain and targets more than 90% by 2030. This
entity is set to become the European leader in the closed-
loop automotive circular economy, on an industrial scale. It
will serve Renault Group and the entire industry.
In order to accelerate its development and strengthen its
leadership, The Future Is NEUTRAL is opening up a minority
of its capital to external investors with the objective of co-
financing investments of around €500 million until 2030. In
October 2024, Renault Group and SUEZ thus announced
their decision to intensify their historic cooperation and
position The Future Is NEUTRAL as a leading player in the
automotive circular economy. SUEZ acquired a 20% stake
in the capital of The Future Is NEUTRAL, alongside
Renault Group, now a co-shareholder with 80%. Under this
deal, The Future Is NEUTRAL receives a €140  million
investment from its two shareholders to accelerate and
expand its activities, to the benefit of the entire European
automotive industry.
 Renault Group    I    Universal registration document 2024
29

## Page 32

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
A performance driver for Renault Group
The Group’s Revolution will see an acceleration of its ESG
trajectory, representing a key driver for the Group’s
operational and financial performance.
The reorganization of Renault Group into entities structured
around value chains allows each entity to have its own ESG
objectives, all of which contribute to the Group’s ESG
ambitions.
In terms of climate, the Group is aiming for carbon neutrality
in Europe by 2040 and worldwide by 2050, by adopting a
cradle-to-grave approach. The Group has set itself
intermediate carbon footprint reduction targets to mark its
path, with specific action plans for each of its activities.
In addition, in keeping with its tradition of social
responsibility, Renault Group will support the transition by
upgrading and training thousands of people to the new
value chains of the automotive revolution. For example, with
ReKnow University, open to the entire supply chain, Renault
Group has trained more than 38,500 employees since 2021 in
future skills required for the automotive industry, in areas
such as electric mobility, circular economy, software and
cybersecurity & data.
Financial outlook
In a market still marked by uncertainty on demand and
regulatory constraints, Renault Group will benefit in 2025
from full year impact of 2024 launches and 2025 product
offensive, combined with the acceleration of cost reduction.
They will be the drivers of operational performance and
sound cash generation.
In 2025, considering market uncertainties especially due to
CO2 emissions regulation impact in Europe (CAFE), Renault
Group is aiming to achieve:
●A Group operating margin ≥7% (it includes around 1 point
of estimated CAFE negative impact).
●A free cash flow ≥ €2bn including €150m of Mobilize
Financial Services (Mobilize F.S.) dividend (versus €600m
in 2024).
●Mobilize F.S. dividend policy is based on a minimum level of
equity to keep complying with both the European Central
Bank and the credit rating agencies solvency ratios.
Therefore, Mobilize F.S. pay-out ratio depends on the level
of
financing
outstandings
and
equity.
Financing
outstandings have strongly increased in 2024 due to the
increase of business and to the sharp rise in average
vehicle prices, leading Mobilize F.S. to consider a dividend
of €150m. From next year, Mobilize F.S. dividends will rise
again to return to a level in line with historical average
(subject to regulatory and Mobilize F.S. board approvals).
Dividend policy
Renault Group has reinstated the payment of a dividend
since 2023 (for the 2022 financial year). This dividend
symbolises a new era. The payout rate will increase
gradually and in a disciplined way to 35% of net income –
group share (subject to approval by the General Meeting of
Shareholders).
Capital allocation policy
Renault Group plans to reinvest at least 50% of the surplus
cash generated within the Group. In the future, Renault
Group wishes to be more active on financial investments, in
line with its ecosystem approach, but by limiting them to a
maximum of 15% to 20% of its free cash flow. 
For the allocation of the remaining cash, apart from the dividend,
the Group wishes to involve employees in its performance to
promote a shared sense of belonging to the project and foster a
culture of value. Through dedicated employee share ownership
programmes, the Group aims to increase employee share
ownership to 10% of the capital by 2030.
The existing bonds remain under Renault S.A., which is the
issuer of the Group’s industrial activities. Each business
could use financing instruments depending on its own needs
and strategy.

The Group’s activities have been organised into three main
types of operating activities in 36 countries:
●Automobile,
with
the
design,
manufacture
and
distribution of products through its sales network
(including its Renault Retail Group subsidiary):
– New vehicles, with several ranges (PC and LCV) marketed
under four brands: Renault, Dacia, Alpine, Mobilize; 
– used vehicles and spare parts; 
– the Renault powertrain range, sold B2B;
●Sales Financing, operated by RCI Banque SA and its
subsidiaries under the trade name of Mobilize Financial
Services (sales financing, rental, maintenance and
service contracts) and
●Mobility Services under the Mobilize Beyond Automotive
brand (flexible, sustainable and innovative mobility and
energy solutions for the benefit of electric vehicle users).

## Page 34

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
1.3.1 Automotive
Renault Group designs, manufactures and sells passenger
cars and light commercial vehicles under three automotive
brands: Renault, Dacia and Alpine.
2024 was a mixed year. The European market (Association
of European Automobile Manufacturers) was slightly
positive at +1.7%. Manufacturers’ portfolios have returned to
normal levels. This positive balance, however, hides
significant gaps between very dynamic markets (Poland
+15%, Spain +8%, Netherlands +16%, UK +3%) and declining
markets (particularly France at -2%). Italy and Germany are
relatively stable.
In Türkiye, the market remained at high levels after two very
strong years (+0.5%).
In South Korea, the market dropped significantly (-8%) due
to
inflationary
pressures
affecting
consumers
and
regulatory transition for commercial vehicles.
In Latin America, Brazil (14%) experienced a strong upturn,
and Argentina (-8%), down over the whole year, saw an
increase in recent months, which should enable 2025 to be
significantly better.

Renault 5 E-Tech electric on

The Renault 5 E-Tech electric
has been on the market since

Renault 4 E-Tech electric: the

internationally.

Kardian is a compact, urban

Rafale is unveiling its high-performance 4×4 300 hp version to the

and efficiency!
Available since June 2024, New Renault Master is designed for
greater distances, charging and savings thanks to a much more
efficient design than its competitors: the perfect next-generation
multi-energy Aerovan.

## Page 36

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
The Renault brand strategy:
Renault, a “pop” and “tech” brand
Renault is constantly innovating to improve mobility. “Pop”
in its offering – human, optimistic and full of life – the Renault
brand is inventing the future of ever more “tech” mobility,
while staying truly human and responsible.
Today, Renault also continues to innovate on the latest
generation of the “car for living” concept, for an ever more
welcoming, intuitively intelligent, warm and safe experience.
To optimise its carbon footprint, the Renault brand has
chosen to replace the leather seats throughout its range
with partially recycled grained-coated fabrics.  
In Europe, the Renault brand performs in the C-segment,
which plays an increasingly important role in its overall
sales, with a 32.6% segment share in 2024, for a total of
230,453 units sold in 2024.
In addition, in Europe*, Renault is maintaining its level of
retail sales at an optimal level, with nearly one in two
vehicles sold to private customers. Retail sales reached
47.8% for the full year 2024.
In 2024, the promotion also continues, outside Europe, with
the expansion of the marketing of Kwid E-Tech electric in
Latin American markets, and the expansion of Taliant into
new markets in the rest of the world. Renault Kardian, made
in Brazil and Morocco, New Renault Duster, made in Türkiye,
and Grand Koleos, made in Korea, are the first building
blocks of the product offensive of the international plan. 
* From Europe Dataforce
At the same time, the Renault brand is continuing its
development in light commercial vehicles.
In 2024, Renault is #1 in the European LCV (Light Commercial
Vehicles) market excluding pick-ups. This leadership is built
around each of the following vehicles:
●Express Van & Kangoo Van enable the Renault brand to
be the European leader in the Small Van segment;
●Trafic Van is on the European podium in the Light Van
segment;
●Master Van is number #3 in Heavy Vans.
With its E-Tech umbrella brand, Renault continues
its offensive on the electrified solution market:
Renault E-Tech covers hybrid, plug-in hybrids and 100%
electric vehicles.
E-Tech technology is available on all the brand’s main
models.
Renault, a pioneer in electric vehicles, remains one of the
major players in electric mobility in Europe.
After Megane and Scenic, it’s R5’s turn to reinvent itself and
become the spearhead of Renault’s new generation of
electric vehicles.
Megane E-Tech electric, Scenic E-Tech electric and R5 E-Tech
electric
embody
the
brand’s
transformation,
with
a
breakthrough that is both emotional and technological.
Connected and integrated into the electric and digital
ecosystem of its users, they allow Renault to continue its
history of “new-generation cars for life and for living”.
Models for 2024
Renault passenger cars (PC)
Renault Europe passenger cars (PC):
Renault, the offensive in segment C continues:
The “C” segment, the most profitable segment on the
market, is still at the heart of Renault’s strategic plan.
After Arkana, Renault continued the offensive with Megane
E-Tech electric, then Renault Austral, Renault Scenic E-Tech
and finally Renault Symbioz.
Megane E-Tech electric:
The fully electric Megane E-Tech was the first model of the
Renaulution to be 100% “made in ElectriCity”, the new
European reference for electric vehicles, set up by Renault
Group in the north of France.
Since its launch, Megane E-Tech electric has gone
international, opening up to 6 countries on 3 continents
(Türkiye,
Australia,
Morocco,
Brazil,
Colombia
and
Argentina).
The fully electric Megane E-Tech is pushing the limits of its
segment for its charging capacity and range, while
continuing to improve its on-board experience. The model
has sold 115,793 vehicles since its launch, with more than 20%
of the mix in the high-end finish, which confirms Renault’s
legitimacy in the electric car segment.
Scenic E-Tech electric:   
The fully electric Scenic E-Tech, made in Douai, was voted
2024 “Car of the Year” in Europe.
It’s Renault’s first fully electric family vehicle, designed to be
sustainable (at least 90% recyclable*, with 25.6%** of its
materials coming from the circular economy).
With its 625  km WLTP range, excellent spaciousness and
OpenR Link system with integrated Google for easier trip
planning, the fully electric Scenic E-Tech allows families to
transition to electric with ease.
Thanks to its qualities, 24,208 vehicles have been sold in
Europe since its launch in late April 2024, with a mix of 58% in
high-end finish and 90% with the long-range battery.
* according to Directive 2005/64/EC
** including recycled materials according to ISO 14021 and production scrap
reintegrated into the manufacturing processes.
Symbioz:
Symbioz is one of the 2024 pillars of the “Renaulution”
strategic plan to win back the C-segment. This latest arrival
continues Renault’s heritage of family-friendly “cars for
living”. This compact, versatile model combines the best in
design,
modularity,
load
capacity,
connectivity
and
efficiency. Aimed at retail family customers as well as
corporate fleet customers.
34

General presentation of Renault Group
01
Compact on the outside, roomy on the inside. In terms of
refinement, its Iconic finish puts it up there with the highest
standards in the C segment. In terms of load capacity,
Renault Symbioz is at the top of its class.
The best of Renault’s technology, acclaimed in the higher
segment, is also available on Symbioz: a full range of
technological equipment including the Solarbay opacifying
sunroof, the OpenR Link multimedia system with integrated
Google, which comes with a 10.4-inch vertical touchscreen
as standard on all versions, and the best of Google with
Google Maps, Google Assistant and Google Play.
Symbioz has the highest level of active and passive safety. It
is equipped with 29 driving aids, including emergency
braking when reversing, for example.
Its E-Tech full hybrid 145  hp engine gives it the comfort and
silence of electric driving in town with no charging constraints.
Made in Valladolid and launched in September in Europe,
Symbioz sold 16,838 vehicles in 2024.
Austral:
Sales of the Austral, made in Palencia, reached 87,824 units
in 2024, up from 2023, notably with the opening of Morocco
and strong progress in fleet sales.
Full-hybrid powertrains accounted for more than 60% of
sales, while the higher trim levels (Esprit Alpine and Iconic)
represented more than 50%. Resale values are at an all-time
high. Significant improvements have been made to
acoustics, dynamic comfort (new shock absorbers) and
safety (GSR2 standard).
Arkana:
Arkana, made in Korea, confirmed its success in 2024,
totalling nearly 320,053 cars sold since its launch.
Like the other models in the Renault range, Arkana is now
offered in a top-of-the-range Esprit Alpine finish.
It continued to expand its international offering in 2024, with
the launch of its mild hybrid versions in Latin America
(Mexico, Colombia) and the Persian Gulf.
In the B-segment
Renault 5 E-Tech electric:
A phenomenal car - avant-garde, versatile and economical –
Renault 5 helped millions of families meet the challenges of
its time: the oil crisis, changing habits and the arrival of a
second car in the household.
True to this legacy of extraordinary modernity, the electric
Renault 5 E-Tech is a bold response to the societal and
environmental challenges of its time – fuel economy,
durability and reduced carbon footprint.
A design that strikes a chord, an experience that is both
pleasurable and made easier thanks to electric and digital
technology, local, responsible, circular production… It has
everything it takes to become the model city car for the
electric transition.
The Renault 5 E-Tech electric has just won the prestigious
title of “Car of the Year 2025”, awarded by the European jury
COTY (Car of the Year).
The Renault 5 E-Tech electric inaugurates a new fully electric
AmpR Small platform developed to enhance its iconic design,
without compromising on performance. It features the very
best in electrical components and modern technology,
offering driving pleasure and comfort that are unrivalled in
the segment, all at the best possible price, not to mention the
integration of a larger battery (52 kWh) for a range of up to
410 kilometres WLTP, despite the car’s compact size.
With a turning circle reduced to 10.3  m, it’s easy to
manoeuvre around town. The multi-link rear axle offers
more vigour on tight bends, greater stability in big curves
and improved comfort.
The Renault 5 E-Tech electric is equipped with a heat pump
for thermal comfort in the passenger compartment while
saving as much energy as possible from the battery.
It’s the first in a long line of cars to be fitted with the new
11 kW two-way AC charger, which offers V2L (vehicle-to-load)
functions to connect a device to the car’s battery and V2G
(vehicle-to-grid) functions to feed electricity back into the
grid and save money on charging at home.
The Renault 5 E-Tech electric have the OpenR Link system
with integrated Google and is the first vehicle to carry the
new Reno virtual mobility companion on board. This Reno
avatar, with a real personality, accompanies and interacts
with Renault customers throughout their user experience,
inside and outside the car.
In terms of drive assistance (ADAS), it is up there with the
best in the segment, both in terms of driving aids and safety
and parking aids.
By the end of December, we had already exceeded
13,203 sales Renault 5 E-Tech electric vehicles in Europe.
Lastly, the Renault 5 E-Tech electric (52 kWh battery version) is
sustainably designed:
●at least 88.6% recyclable*
●including 26.4%** of materials from the circular economy
●with fabrics up to 100% recycled from plastic bottles for
seat textiles on the Techno and Iconic Cinq versions.
* according to Directive 2005/64/EC.
** including recycled materials according to ISO 14021 and production scrap
reintegrated into the manufacturing processes.
 Renault Group    I    Universal registration document 2024
35

## Page 38

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
Renault 4 E-Tech electric:
The Renault 4 E-Tech electric draws on the DNA of the original
model and returns as a versatile compact car for today.
The electric Renault 4 will be released for sale in spring 2025.
The brand offers a range of fully electric vehicles to suit all needs
and uses. The Renault 4 E-Tech electric will be right up there
in the B-segment, just above the Renault 5 E-Tech electric.
Clio:
In 2024, Clio ranked 6th in worldwide sales in the B-segment,
with 302,756 vehicles sold.
Clio is second in Europe ACEA with 231,899 (+5.8% vs. 2023)
across all segments, including 57,000 E-Tech hybrid vehicles.
Clio remains the best-selling model in France with
102,790  registrations in 2024. The successful launch of the
new Clio in 2023 breathed new life into the vehicle, with a
flagship version in particular: the Esprit Alpine E-Tech hybrid
145 hp version.
New Captur:
Versatile and multi-talented, Captur is both compact on the
outside and liveable on the inside, comfortable in the city
and off the beaten track. These qualities explain its
commercial success since its debut 10 years ago, with more
than 2 million units sold in 90 countries worldwide.
Following in the footsteps of New Clio, New Renault Captur is
now embodying the brand’s new design language, with a
completely new, more status-enhancing front end.
In a market where urban SUVs now account for around 50%
of B-segment sales, New Renault Captur intends to remain a
reference model by retaining its versatility of use, its range
of two-tone bodywork, its top-level interior space and its
ingenious technological equipment. In addition to these
qualities, which are part of Captur’s DNA, this new
generation boasts new assets.
The more expressive exterior and interior styling is
transformed into a unique combination of elegance and
sportiness with the new Esprit Alpine finish.
The cabin is enhanced by new upholstery and screens.
Available depending on the version, the OpenR Link
multimedia system with integrated Google offers the best in
on-board technology with Android Automotive 12, a world
first in the B-segment.
Thanks to an entirely new electronic architecture, safety is
at the highest level with the latest generation of driving aids,
including predictive hybrid driving and Active Driver Assist,
for level 2 driver assistance.
Depending on the country where it is sold, New Renault
Captur is available with five engine types, including the
145  hp E-Tech hybrid, which is now the preferred choice of
more than 35% of customers. To enhance driving pleasure
and provide more dynamic handling, the suspension and
power steering calibration have been revised. Available
depending on the version, the Extended Grip system
optimises traction in all road conditions.
Still produced at the Valladolid plant, New Renault Captur
has been on sale since April 2024 and totals 158,104 sales in
2024, keeping it among the leaders in its category.
Kangoo PC:
Kangoo, available in a 5-seater version, and its extended
version Grand Kangoo (5- and 7-seater) are produced in
Maubeuge (France). They are available in both internal
combustion (blue dCi  95 and TCe  130) and electric (90  kW
electric motor for a range of up to 285 km WLTP) versions.
Kangoo and Grand Kangoo are appreciated for their
superior transport capacity and modularity, with a flexible
interior layout. The 7-seater version is particularly appealing
for large families or companies requiring passenger
transport. The seats can be folded or removed to adapt the
space as needed, offering great versatility.
With sliding doors on both sides, Grand Kangoo makes it
easier to access the rear seats in tight parking spaces. This
makes it very practical for families compared with vehicles
with conventional doors.
The low loading sill and large boot opening also simplify
loading and unloading, which is an advantage for
professional users and families who often carry bulky items.
In the D and E segments
Espace E-Tech hybrid:
In 2024, Espace, manufactured in Palencia, had its first full
year of sales in all its markets, with 21,091 units sold. It is neck
and neck with its main competitor in France.
In June 2024, Espace received a major update, focusing on
safety, which was rewarded with the maximum 5-star rating
from EuroNCAP.
In keeping with the brand’s commitments, Espace replaced
the leather seats in its Iconic finish in November with seats in
TERRA “grained coated fabric”, perforated on the seat area
and backrest areas, with a recycled backing, offering a
choice of black in addition to light sandy grey, and a more
enveloping design for the front seats.
Rafale E-Tech:
Manufactured in Palencia, Rafale is an emotional SUV coupe
that complements the more rational, modular Renault
Espace, available in 5- and 7-seat configurations. With
unique technological content, the 200  hp Rafale E-Tech
hybrid went on sale in June, and the 300  hp Rafale E-Tech
4×4 plug-in hybrid went on sale in November.

General presentation of Renault Group
01
Rafale was designed entirely around the notion of pleasure.
First and foremost, it’s a feast for the eyes, with a sleek,
elegant design and the highest levels of perceived quality.
Then there’s the pleasure of travelling, with the best rear-
seat space in the SUV coupe market and iconic features,
such as the Esprit Alpine seats, the Solarbay and the smart
rear armrest. Finally, the driving experience is enhanced by
4  cm wider tracks, specific settings for the 4Control
Advanced system and reworked, more direct steering for
even greater sportiness. Driving pleasure is magnified with
E-Tech 4×4 plug-in hybrid (300 hp). It is the only mainstream
model to offer the combination of four-wheel steering and
four-wheel drive. And the Rafale E-Tech 4×4 300 hp Atelier
Alpine version goes even further with a chassis completely
overhauled by Alpine engineers and innovations such as
Multi-Sense Chassis Control, which incorporates all of
Alpine’s expertise in 3 modes: comfort, dynamic and sport,
as well as the development of camera-based road-reading
technology to manage predictive damping.
Rafale stands out not only for its dynamic performance, but
also for its market-leading efficiency: range between 1,000
and 1,100  km, depending on the version and some of the
lowest fuel consumption figures on the SUV coupe market.
By the end of December, we had already sold 12,742 in
Europe. The high-end E-Tech 4x4 300 hp version has
achieved a very high level of mix with 40% since its launch in
November 2024.
Trafic PC:
Trafic Combi can carry up to 9 people, offering a spacious
and
adaptable
interior,
ideal
for
people
transport
professionals or large families. It combines comfort, modern
equipment and economical engine options for urban and
intercity journeys trips.
Trafic SpaceClass – designed to carry VIP customers – is a
mobile lounge for an enhanced on-board experience,
offering up to 6 face-to-face seats, individual sliding,
pivoting and removable seats, a sliding table, etc.
Trafic Spacenomad is the result of Renault Group’s
collaboration with the French specialist “GROUPE PILOTE”.
In its top-of-the-range version, it is fully equipped, including
with a solar panel as standard. Spacenomad went on sale in
France and Switzerland in 2024.
Renault passenger cars (PC) – OUTSIDE Europe:
In the A-segment and B-segment
Kwid E-Tech:
Launched in Brazil in 2022, the fully electric Kwid E-Tech was
voted winner of the “Best Resale 2024” award in April 2024
by Quatro Rodas, in partnership with Kelley Blue Book (KBB),
and by the same Quatro Rodas as winner of the “lowest
operating cost” award in June 2024. Sales of the 100%
electric Kwid E-Tech in 2024 totalled 2,389 units.
The internal combustion version of the Kwid also won the
“clean mobility” award in July 2024 and sold 96,536 units in
2024 in Latin America.
Duster (Latin America):
Duster, made in Brazil and Colombia, was brought to life this
year with a model year that introduces, in addition to a
number of design modifications such as new full LED lighting
equipment, 6 airbags and some additional equipment such
as Cornering Light, Follow Me Home, Rain Sensor and
Wireless Charger.
Launched in Brazil in January 2024 and in Colombia,
Argentina and Mexico in the second quarter of 2024, Duster
sold a total of 34,817 units in Latin America in 2024.
New Duster (made in Türkiye):
This new opus “made in Türkiye” (Bursa plant) continues the
legacy of this true icon with an all-new design, more
technology and modern powertrains.
Produced on the CMF-B platform, also used for Clio and
Captur, the new Renault Duster benefits from high-tech
multimedia equipment and modern engines such as the
E-Tech hybrid or the mild hybrid advanced. It fully embodies
the hallmarks of the Renault brand.
Launched in Türkiye at the end of July 2024, it will gradually
be rolled out to the rest of the world: 1st quarter 2025 in
Ukraine, Egypt, South Africa and more widely throughout
Africa, then in the 2nd half in the Gulf countries, Saudi Arabia,
Australia and Kazakhstan.
In India:
In early 2024, the Kwid, Triber and Kiger models came to life:
Kwid has been enhanced with a touchscreen on the E2 version
and a rear seat belt.
Total sales of the model reached 8,705 units in India in 2024.
Triber has been enhanced with a 7-inch TFT display and a
wireless charger.
Total sales of the model reached 22,024 units in India in 2024.
Kiger has been enhanced with an interior dimming mirror
and cruise control.
Total sales of the model reached 11,000 units in India in 2024.
The year was also marked by the launch of the “Night & Day”
limited edition for the Diwali festival of lights on Kwid, Triber
& Kiger. This limited edition comes with a two-tone roof and
a 9-inch screen. The “Night & Day” limited edition has been
enthusiastically received by the public. The entire inventory
of this limited edition sold out in 40 days.
Kardian (B segment):
This groundbreaking model is the first in a complete future
range of vehicles destined to cross borders, illustrating the
brand’s new strategy outside Europe. Designed to meet market
expectations as closely as possible and the result of close
collaboration between teams based in the countries where
Renault has historical roots and the Technocentre teams.
 Renault Group    I    Universal registration document 2024
37

## Page 40

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
This urban SUV was first launched in Brazil in March 2024.
The model was gradually rolled out across Latin America,
particularly in Mexico in June 2024, Colombia in July 2024 and
Argentina in August 2024. In total, no fewer than 10 countries
are already sourced from our factory in Curitiba (Brazil).
Kardian also began its industrialisation at the Casablanca
plant (SOMACA) in Morocco.
Kardian is a compact, urban B-segment SUV. Its styling and
equipment reflect the Renault DNA at the heart of its design.
On its front end, it sports the brand’s new stylistic identity
with the “Nouvel’R” logo and a new brand light signature.
Resolutely modern, it boasts innovations and features
usually reserved for the top segment, such as 17-inch wheels,
a high console with an e-shifter gear lever and Multi-Sense
settings that influence the driving experience and on-board
ambience. Kardian also has 6 airbags and 13 driving support
systems (ADAS), including blind spot warning and the multi-
view camera.
Kardian marks the launch of Renault Group’s new ultra-
versatile modular platform, which will be used to develop a
wide range of models for many markets outside Europe.
Kardian is powered by an engine produced in Brazil. It’s a
3-cylinder 1.0-litre turbocharged petrol engine coupled
with an all-new EDC dual-clutch automatic gearbox.
The model is recognised as a game changer in the highly
competitive B-SUV segment. In Brazil alone, it has already
received 11 awards, including “Car of the Year” from Auto
Esporte, the Quatro Rodas comparison, Motor show, and
Jornal Do Carro. It was also voted “Auto Del Año” in
Argentina by Grupo Premia.
In 2024, 31,749 Kardians were sold, with Brazil as its leading
market (77% of sales).
Taliant (B-segment sedan):
In March 2024, Taliant benefited from a product upgrade with
the introduction of 2 new body colours—shale grey and urban
grey—and a new 16“ diamond-cut rim for its high version.
Sales in 2024 are mainly concentrated in Türkiye, with 79% of
the 7,878 units sold in total.
This model will become even more international with the
start of exports to Ukraine, then to the Gulf countries as
announced at the Riyadh Motor Show in 2023.
In the C segment
Megane Saloon:
In 2024, Megane Saloon continued its international sales
performance beyond Türkiye, with a total volume of
46,181 units.
With 40,077 sales in Türkiye, it generated nearly 87% of its
volumes there, ranking second in its segment.
Express:
Express, manufactured in Tangier in Morocco, also supports
Renault’s position in the Combispace segment in certain
markets outside Europe.
Express is notably the best-selling model in Morocco with
11,013 sales in 2024.
In the D and E segments
Grand Koleos:
Grand Koleos, made in Korea, has ideal proportions for a
D-segment SUV, with generous space for five passengers.
It was launched in September 2024 in South Korea.
A distinctive vehicle with sculpted, elegant lines, it embodies
the Renault brand’s new design language. Alongside the
dashboard’s large horizontal screen, the openR panorama
screen introduces a new, more intuitive and personalised
approach to travel.
Grand Koleos also has 31 advanced driver assistance
systems
(ADAS),
three
of
which
feature
innovative
autonomous driving functions.
Grand Koleos introduces a new version of the E-Tech hybrid
powertrain, specifically designed for premium vehicles on
the global market. Offering first-class electric performance,
this 245 hp powertrain includes a 106 kW combustion engine
and an exceptionally powerful 100 kW traction motor. Grand
Koleos also has two turbo petrol engines, 210 hp 2WD 7DCT
and 210 hp 4WD 8AT.
In South Korea, 22,034 Grand Koleos were sold in 2024.
Koleos will expand its international presence, starting with
the Gulf countries in early 2025, followed by expansion in
Latin American countries throughout the year.
Renault Light Commercial Vehicles (LCVs):
In 2024, Renault’s sales performance evolved in a difficult
economic climate. Sales of light commercial vehicles
amounted to 402,971, representing a 6.2% global market
share (excluding North America, China and Russia).
In Europe, the Renault brand is in the top 2 with 15.3% of the
LCV market. Renault is also in the top 2 market leader,
excluding pick-ups, in the four main Latin American markets.
The Renault LCV range covers all customer needs and
market segments, with both internal combustion and fully
electric versions, up to 22 m3. Renault is one of Europe’s top
three sellers of electric LCVs, with a 10.8% share of the LCV
market.
Kangoo Van:
The Kangoo Van, manufactured at the Maubeuge plant
(France), boasts the best dimensions in its category with a
loading volume of 3.3  m3 to 3.9  m3 in the L1 version; from
4.3  m3 to 4.9  m3 and up to 3.5 metres of effective loading
length on the ground (with pivoting mesh partition and
folded passenger seat) for the L2 long-wheelbase version.
Kangoo Van, voted Van of the Year 2022 in both internal
combustion and electric versions, features a number of
innovations to help our customers carry out a wide range of
activities (the best side opening in the segment, “Open
Sesame by Renault” (1.45 m), retractable interior rack, etc.).

01
Kangoo Van E-Tech offers one of the best driving ranges in
the small commercial vehicle segment with a range of up to
300 km. Kangoo Van E-Tech is the leader in Europe with an
18.8% share of the electric Small Van segment.
Trafic:
Trafic is a benchmark multipurpose van in the segment,
ever more comfortable and a real mobile office, suited to the
multiple expectations and uses of business customers.
Trafic has record dimensions in effective length (4.15 m) and
a volume ranging from 5.2 to 8.6 m3. It is available in many
versions, from the panel van to the platform cab, both in an
internal combustion version and, since the end of 2023, also
in a 100% electric version.
With the Trafic Van E-Tech, no compromises have been made
compared with the combustion-engine version by offering a
high level of performance, diversity and customisation.
Trafic is in Europe’s top 3 in the Light Van segment (with a
segment share of 17%). It is manufactured at the Sandouville
plant in France.
Master:
Renault is in the top 3 of the Heavy Van segment in Europe.
The launch of New Master in June 2024 should enable us to
strengthen our position in the market. New Master is marked
by its efficiency thanks to its “Aerovan” aerodynamic
design as well as its new dynamic braking system and
optimisation work on the engines.
New Master therefore consumes 1.5 litres/100 km (internal
combustion) and 21 kWh/100 km (electric) less than the
previous version of Master. This gives New Master the best
range in the segment, with up to 460  km of WLTP electric
range on the N2 (4T) version with an 87 kW battery.
New Master also builds on the strengths that made the
previous generation such a success: New Master comes in a
wide range of versions, with three lengths, three heights
(van, floor and chassis cab), front-wheel drive and rear-
wheel drive, factory conversions such as steel tippers and
flatbeds, crew cabs, high-capacity models, covering 11 to
22 m3 of effective volume.
New Master is manufactured at the Batilly plant in France.
The previous generation of Master 3 (X62) is manufactured
at the Curitiba plant in Brazil.
Pick-ups:
For Renault, the pick-up market offers the opportunity to win
new customers outside Europe. Alaskan and Oroch form
Renault’s offering for markets outside Europe. In Latin
America, Alaskan is marketed in Argentina, Colombia and
Central American countries (importers).
Oroch was renewed in early 2022 and represents
21,504  sales in 2024. Oroch ranks #1 in Colombia, #4 in
Mexico, #4 in Argentina, and #5 in Brazil.

## Page 42

Renault Group
General presentation of Renault Group
Brand strategy
Founded in 1968 then launched from 2004 all across Europe
and Mediterranean countries, Dacia has always offered the
best value-for-money cars by constantly redefining the
essentials. Dacia is a game changer offering simple,
multipurpose, reliable cars in tune with its customers’
lifestyles. Dacia models became a reference on the market:
Sandero, the best-selling car for European consumers;
Duster, the most affordable SUV; Jogger, the 5- or 7-seater
family model; and Spring, the champion of accessible
electric mobility in Europe. Dacia is a brand of Renault
Group, with a presence in 44 countries. Since 2004, it has
sold more than 9 million vehicles.
In 1999, guided by Louis Schweitzer, Dacia joined Renault
Group and opened up to new ambitions.
This turning point in the brand’s history came in 2004 with
the launch of Logan, a modern, robust and, above all,
affordable family sedan. Initially designed for emerging
markets, at an unbeatable price of €5,000, it was a great
commercial success, including in Western Europe, where it
went on sale in 2005. A new vehicle at a second-hand price,
it was a revolution in the car market.
2008 saw the arrival of the Sandero, Dacia’s second major
launch under Renault Group. It also proved to be its biggest
commercial success. Its many advantages – an interior
volume worthy of a higher segment, a practical, versatile
spirit and an affordable price – have made it the top-selling
car for individuals in Europe since 2017.
In 2010, Dacia launched the Duster, an attractive vehicle with
true off-road capabilities.
In 2024, the brand presented and released New Spring, the
most affordable electric vehicle on the market in Europe.
In October, New Bigster, the brand’s latest vehicle, offers the
best cost-to-performance ratio in the C-SUV segment. This
vehicle offers greater comfort, technology and load
capacity, with a 4×4 version for the most demanding drivers.
Last but not least, Dacia, which is launching into motor sport,
took part in the Dakar rally in January 2025. In 2024, the
brand introduced the Sandrider vehicle, which has been
tried and tested by top drivers (Sébastien Loeb, Nasser
Al-Attiyah and Cristina Gutierrez-Herrero). In October, this
vehicle won a spectacular victory at the Rallye du Maroc.
Nasser Al-Attiyah finished 4th in the Dakar 2025.

01
Dacia today and tomorrow:
Dacia will always be Dacia
Dacia continues to write its own script, without ever
forgetting what has made it a success: the guarantee of
unbeatable value for money on all its vehicles. From design
to sales, manufacturing and transport, the brand remains
faithful to its strategy of optimising costs at every step so
that its customers pay only for what they need.
Once again this year, Dacia continued to assert its 3 brand
values:
●Both essential and cool at the same time
Dacia
is
ingenious,
creative
and
attractive,
with
innovations such as Media Control or modular roof bars.
This is also true in terms of design, with a cool look… and at
no extra cost;
●Robust and outdoor
Reliability and robustness are among the attributes on
which Dacia has built its success. These attributes make
Dacia cars ideal for outdoor activities;
●Eco-smart – economical and eco-friendly
Dacia is committed to reconciling the interests of
individuals with those of society as a whole. Eliminating
the superfluous thus reduces weight and thus saves fuel
and emits less CO2.
In 2024, Dacia achieved a record market share
and remains on the European podium in terms
of private customers
Dacia totalled 676,340 sales in 2024, a 2.7% increase
compared with 2023. The brand crossed the bar of 9 million
vehicles sold since 2004.
This performance enables Dacia to achieve record market
shares in Europe:
●PC+ LCV at 3.9% (+0.1 pt vs. 2023);
●PC at 4.5% (+0.1 pt vs. 2023).
The brand also maintains its place on the European podium for
sales to retail customers, its core clientele, with a market share
of 8.2% (-0.1 pt vs. 2023).
Models for 2024
●Sandero: 309,392 units sold, up +14.5% vs. 2023, the best-
selling vehicle in Europe for all channels combined, and
the number 1 vehicle sold in the retail market in Europe
since 2017.
●Duster (generation 2 and 3): 215,024 units sold, up +7.3% vs.
2023, number 1 SUV sold in the retail market in Europe
since 2018 and number 2 vehicle sold in the retail market
in Europe behind Sandero.
●Jogger: 96,440 units sold, up +2.4% vs. 2023), number 2
vehicle in the C-segment (excluding SUVs) sold in the
retail market in Europe.
●Spring: 22,884 units, down -63% vs. 2023. Sales fell
significantly in 2024, mainly due to the reduction in
government grants. The arrival of the new version in
showrooms in September led to an increase in order over
the last three months.
●Bigster: introduced in 2024, Dacia’s new SUV will be
available in the first half of 2025.
